medications.concept: Ferrous sulfate 325 mg oral tablet — 1 tab daily (iron deficiency)
Atorvastatin 10 mg oral tablet — 1 tab nightly (borderline LDL/ApoB)
Cholecalciferol (vitamin D3) 2000 IU oral — 1 daily (vitamin D insufficiency)

meta.disclaimer: SAMPLE / SYNTHETIC DATA — NOT A REAL PERSON. 'Alex Rivera' is a fictional persona; every value here was generated deterministically for demonstration only and must never be read as medical fact.

persona.name: Alex Rivera
persona.summary: Fictional 45-year-old on a health push: mild iron deficiency (recovering on a supplement) behind afternoon fatigue, improving body composition and resting HR from a strength + Zone-2 program, deep sleep that dips on late-training nights, and borderline lipids trending back toward target.

notes.author: Dr. Priya Nguyen, MD
notes.text: 45 y/o presents for annual visit, reports several months of afternoon fatigue with otherwise reasonable diet and activity. Exam unremarkable, vitals normal. Ordered CBC, iron studies, CMP, lipid panel, HbA1c, vitamin D, TSH, hs-CRP. Results show ferritin low at 22 with low iron saturation (11%) and low-normal hemoglobin — consistent with early iron deficiency without anemia, a plausible driver of the fatigue. Vitamin D insufficient (24). LDL and ApoB borderline elevated; HbA1c and TSH normal. Plan: start ferrous sulfate 325 mg daily with vitamin C, cholecalciferol 2000 IU daily, low-dose atorvastatin 10 mg for the lipid picture. Discussed a strength + Zone-2 cardio program and higher dietary protein. Recheck iron studies and lipids in ~12 weeks.

notes.author: Dr. Priya Nguyen, MD
notes.text: Iron-studies and lipid recheck. Ferritin improved 22 → 29 (still marginally low), iron saturation 11 → 18%, hemoglobin 13.6 → 13.9. Reports afternoon energy better on most days, but notably worse the mornings after late evening workouts, which also show shorter deep sleep on his watch data. Body composition improving on the program (body fat ~22% → ~19%, weight down ~2.5 kg, resting HR trending down). Lipids improving: LDL 135 → 128, ApoB 105 → 98 — continue atorvastatin and lifestyle, recheck in 3 months. Vitamin D 24 → 31 — continue D3. Counseled to shift weeknight training earlier to protect deep sleep. Continue iron; recheck ferritin in 8-10 weeks.

questions.label: Why am I tired in the afternoons?

subsystems.driver: Deep sleep & HRV up — but dip on late-training nights
Resting HR ↓, body fat ↓, muscle ↑, steps ↑
